rulings.Case Title: ANN REBUCAS VS. BASIL UGONNA NJOKU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Determine Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT JUAN RUDOLPH, DOMINGO CANTOR AND CLARITA CANTOR'S Motion To Determine Good Faith Settlement IS GRANTED AS CONSISTENT WITH TECH-BILT. REBUCAS' TESTIMONY ESTABLISHES THE BASIS FOR THE ALLOCATION AND THE ARBITRATOR'S DECISION, BASED ONLY ON THE POLICE REPORT, IS NOT PERSUASIVE ON THE ALLOCATION OF FAULT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WOLLBORG/MICHELSON PERSONNEL SERVICE INC VS. EXECUTIVE RISK INDEMNITY INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 7, PLAINTIFF WOLLBORG/MICHELSON PERSONNEL SERVICE INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES THREE, FOUR, FIVE, TWELVE AND THIRTEEN BASED ON HAMILTON V MARYLAND (2002) 27 CAL. 4TH 718, 728. OVERRULE DEMURRER AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES SIX, SEVEN AND EIGHT BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS. OVERRULE DEMURRER AS TO THE NINTH AND ELEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS CURED THE UNCERTAINTY. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 8. CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT K.S.L.O. IS A PROPER PLAINTIFF GIVEN THAT AGUILAR PAID THE ENTIRE DEBT. THE COURT ALSO ORDERS THE CONSOLIDATION FOR ACTION 458849 WITH 455712. CASE 455712 IS DESIGNATED AS THE LEAD CASE. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 8. CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI'S Motion To Strike Portions Of CROSS-Complaint: MOTION TO STRIKE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: E. M. MCCARTT VS. PENSCO PENSION SERVICES, INC., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT PENSCO PENSION SERVICES, INC. AND PENSCO, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PENSCO PENSION SERVICES. DEMURRER TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS MOOT BECAUSE IT HAS BEEN DISMISSED. OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON PARAGRAPH SEVEN. THE ISSUES RELATING TO THE EFFECT OF THE DIRECTION LETTER CANNOT BE ADDRESSED ON DEMURRER GIVEN THE ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPH SEVEN. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: DEFEND BAYWIEW HUNTERS POINT COMMITTEE AN VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 12. PETITIONER DEFEND BAYWIEW HUNTERS POINT COMMITTEE'S Motion For Writ Of Mandamus: A HEARING IS REQUIRED TO ADDRESS THE HOLDING IN WeCare-SANTA PAULA V HERRERA (2006) 139 CAL. rulings.Case Title: LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC VS. RAMONA PINKERMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 13. DEFENDANT RAMONA PINKERMAN'S MOTION For Attorney'S Fees And Costs IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS THE PREVAILING PARTY UNDER SECTION 37.9(F) OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO COSTS OF $578.75 AND REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES OF $6,399.00. THE FEE REQUEST RELATED TO THE DEMURRER AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS REDUCED BY FIFTY PER CENT AS EXCESSIVE TIME SPENT ON A SIMPLE MATTER. $2,000.00 IS ALLOWED FOR PREPARATION OF THE REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: 215 SECOND STREET LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. MASOUD T. MOSTOFI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Have Judgment Entered Pursuant To Stip For Judgment; Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 7, 2007, LINE 14. PLAINTIFF 215 SECOND STREET L.L.C. AND 1277 MISSION STREET, L.L.C.'S Motion To Have Judgment Entered Pursuant To Stipulation For Judgment; Request For Judicial Notice IS DENIED. THE WRITTEN STIPULATION FAILS TO REQUIRE PAYMENT OF RENT ON THE FIRST OF THE MONTH, AND SINCE PLAINTIFF PREPARED IT, ANY CONTRARY INTERPRETATION IS CONSTRUED AGAINST PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/PB)
